TWEET 1
NVIDIA just launched NemoClaw at GTC 2026 — an enterprise AI agent platform built on the open-source OpenClaw framework. Agents running on your own hardware. Governance built in. The enterprise agent stack just arrived.

---

TWEET 2
NemoClaw lets companies build and run AI agents locally. No data leaving your infrastructure. This directly addresses the two biggest objections CTOs raise: security and control.

---

TWEET 3
NVIDIA is also betting inference is the next massive revenue opportunity. As agents execute tasks in real time, inference compute demand will far exceed training. NVIDIA wants to own that entire stack.

---

TWEET 4
IBM expanded its NVIDIA collaboration at GTC — focused on sovereign AI with regional data residency. Enterprise AI that runs within regulatory boundaries without compromising performance.

---

TWEET 5
The pattern is clear: NVIDIA is building the hardware, the agent platform, and the compliance ecosystem simultaneously. They are not selling chips — they are selling the enterprise AI operating layer.

## Fact-check notes
- NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang announced NemoClaw, an enterprise-grade AI agent platform built on the open-source OpenClaw framework, during the GTC 2026 keynote (Source: TechCrunch, March 16, 2026)
- NemoClaw enables enterprises to build and run AI agents locally on their own hardware (Source: TechCrunch, March 16, 2026)
- NVIDIA highlighted AI inference as a major chip revenue opportunity at GTC 2026 (Source: Reuters, March 16, 2026)
- NVIDIA GTC 2026 revealed the Vera Rubin architecture along with updated AI software platforms (Source: SaasNik / NVIDIA GTC, March 16, 2026)
- IBM announced expanded collaboration with NVIDIA at GTC 2026 to advance enterprise AI, including sovereign AI infrastructure with regional data residency (Source: IBM Newsroom, March 16, 2026)
